figure.title: End-to-End Routing through a Switch
figure.purpose: Shows the detailed UALink stack flow for routing a transaction from ACC A to ACC B through a Switch Core.

technical_summary: ACC A connects to SWITCH via UALink PL+SERDES.
SWITCH receives data, unpacks it through UALink DL A and UALink TL A to UPLI Protocol signals.
SWITCH CORE routes the transaction from Port A to Port B.
SWITCH Port B packs the UPLI Protocol signals through UALink TL B and UALink DL B to UALink PL+SERDES B.
SWITCH Port B connects to ACC B via UALink PL+SERDES.

components.name: SWITCH
components.description: Routing switch with multiple UALink stacks (A and B)

components.name: SWITCH CORE
components.description: Core routing logic inside the switch

critical_facts: The Switch Core routes transactions at the UPLI protocol level after TL extraction.
